Before collection of specimen, aspirate sufficient amount of liquid heparin 1000 U/mL into a 2.5 mL syringe.

For assessment of secondary fibrinolysis in suspected venous thrombotic embolism (VTE) and monitoring of post-operative thrombotic risk.

Elevated levels of D-Dimers, the fibrinolytic degradation products of crosslinked fibrin, are associated with venous thromboembolic disorders such as disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC), deep venous thrombosis (DVT) and pulmonary embolism (PE).
Increased D-Dimer levels have also be used as a predictive indicator in an acute myocardial infarction.

Investigation of prolonged APTT. Coagulation inhibitors affecting the APTT may be immediate acting or time dependent. Test plasma containing an immediate acting inhibitor will, when mixed with normal plasma, show little or no correction of the clotting time whereas time dependent inhibitors require a period incubation with normal plasma before they can be detected.

Platelet <50x10e9/L with diffuse microvascular/mucosal bleeding, major bleeding or before invasive procedures 4. Platelet < 100 x 10e9/L with active bleeding in postcardiopulmonary bypass, or with retinal or CNS bleeding/surgery 5. Platelet<50x10e9/L in stable premature neonates or platelet < 100 x 10e9/L in sick premature neonates 6. Suspected platelet dysfunction with active bleeding or before invasive procedures
